data.title: US Senate pushes 30‑month freeze on advanced Nvidia AI chips going to China
data.description:
The Senate moved on Thursday to block Nvidia from sending its most advanced AI chips to China, according to the Financial Times. Lawmakers rolled out a bipartisan bill that would force a 30-month halt on any export approvals for the company’s H200 and Blackwell chips. The whole point is to stop Beijing from getting hardware […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/us-senate-no-advanced-nvidia-chips-to-china/

data.title: U.S. Court Orders OpenAI to Disclose ChatGPT User Data in Copyright Case
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, a U.S. federal judge, Ona T. Wang, has denied OpenAI's request to limit evidence disclosure, mandating the company to provide The New York Times with approximately 20 million anonymized ChatGPT user conversation records. The court deemed these records crucial for determining whether ChatGPT replicated content protected by The New York Times' copyright and found them proportionate to the case's needs.

Despite OpenAI's concerns over user privacy, the judge emphasized that privacy considerations are merely one factor in the proportionality analysis and cannot outweigh the clear relevance and minimal burden of the data in question.

data.title: Solana Co-Founder Discusses Future of Crypto Market Valuation
data.description: According to Foresight News, Solana co-founder Toly recently shared his views on the valuation of the cryptocurrency industry. He stated that high valuation multiples reflect the inherent risks and opportunities within the sector. Toly expressed his belief that the total market capitalization of the crypto market will continue to rise, eventually leading to market share distribution based on revenue. He emphasized that achieving this goal will involve a prolonged and challenging battle for market share, with only those blockchains aiming to dominate the entire market likely to survive.

data.title: U.S. Core PCE Price Index for September to Be Released
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Department of Commerce is set to release the annual rate of the Core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index for September. The announcement is scheduled for Friday at 23:00 UTC+8. The expected rate is 2.9%, consistent with the previous value of 2.9%.

data.title: XRP Panic Levels Reach October Highs, Potential Opportunity Arises
data.description: According to Foresight News, Santiment has tweeted that social data indicates a significant rise in panic sentiment surrounding XRP, which has dropped 31% over the past two months. This level of panic was last observed on November 21, when XRP's price surged by 22% in the following three days. However, the rally was short-lived as greed took over. Currently, a similar opportunity appears to be emerging, reminiscent of the situation two weeks ago.

data.title: Ethereum Coils For A Breakout As IH&S + Heavy Accumulation Emerges
data.description:
Ethereum is approaching a critical moment as multiple bullish signals begin to align. A clear Inverse Head & Shoulders formation, combined with rising accumulation and weakening trend rejection, suggests that the market may be gearing up for a powerful upside move. With momentum tightening and key levels coming into focus, ETH now stands on the verge of a breakout that could set the stage for its next major rally. Inverse Head And Shoulders Signals Brewing Momentum According to a recent update shared by crypto analyst Donald Dean, Ethereum may be gearing up for a significant move
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/ethereum/ethereum-coils-for-a-breakout/

data.title: Strategy won’t be forced to sell Bitcoin if stock drops, Bitwise CIO says
